title: Harden CKC linked-media Argus readouts and actor controls
operator_request: Capture the CKC media UI gaps found during MT-010: Argus needs structured selected album/media/folder/source URL readouts and a steerable media actor identity, not just painted preview text.

state_recovery.recovery_rule: Resume by proving Argus can inspect structured values, not OCR-painted preview text. Do not claim media row steerability from visible labels alone.

scope.summary: Expose selected CKC media state and writer actor identity as stable AccessKit/Argus controls so parallel models can inspect, steer, and attribute album/media work.
base_scope: Add stable author_ids for selected album ref, selected media ref, selected folder ref, selected source URL ref, selected media status, and CKC media actor identity.
Render each selected ref as an AccessKit value-bearing node, not only as painted text in the preview box.
Make the CKC media actor identity steerable by Argus and use it for album create, album link, media notes/tags save, and any local fallback status.
Add clear status text for empty asset ID input, missing selected album, backend unavailable/local fallback, and backend pending states.
Update the internal UserManual and structured agent tool rows with the new readout/control author_ids.
acceptance_criteria: Argus inspect returns stable nodes and values for selected album/media/folder/source URL/status without relying on screenshot OCR.
Argus set_value can set the CKC media actor identity and later write requests use that actor instead of silently falling back to the client default.
Argus click on a media row updates every selected readout to the clicked media occurrence.
Local fallback statuses explicitly say local/non-persistent or degraded when no backend is available.
Character sheet notes remain unchanged when media actor, album, or media notes controls are used.
risks_and_controls: Risk: models read painted preview text as if it were structured state. Control: emit value-bearing AccessKit nodes and test snapshot values directly.
Risk: parallel agents overwrite each other's attribution through a shared client actor. Control: make the actor field steerable and include actor in request/status tests.
Risk: local fallback looks persisted. Control: status text must name local fallback and manual must tell models not to treat it as backend authority.
verification: Frontend Argus snapshot test for selected media readout values.
Frontend Argus steering test for actor field and local/backend status text.
Manual content test or direct manual row assertion covering the new controls.

handoff.note: Created after MT-010 review exposed missing structured selected-media readouts and media actor controls.
